text.title: Approval Token as Signed JWT Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of gov-approval-token-signed-jwt. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Approval tokens signed with HS256; same key on issuer + verifier; key leak forges tokens.
antipattern.fix: Use ES256/EdDSA; private key in HSM; verifier holds public key only.

antipattern.symptom: Token claims `scope: *`; one token can deploy any service.
antipattern.fix: Reject wildcard claims; scope catalog allowlist.

antipattern.symptom: Token TTL = 24h; agent replays the same token across 30 deploys.
antipattern.fix: Mandate `jti` + ≤15min expiry; verifier consumes `jti` on first use.
